description: Grapefruit juice inhibits the CYP3A4 enzyme, leading to increased levels of atorvastatin in the body and a higher risk of muscle damage (myopathy).
NSAIDs like ibuprofen may decrease the blood pressure lowering effects of lisinopril and increase the risk of kidney impairment.
Both drugs prevent clotting. Co-administration increases the risk of internal bleeding and bruising.
Iodinated contrast agents used during imaging can cause acute kidney injury, leading to accumulation of Metformin and potential lactic acidosis.
Propranolol (a beta-blocker) can prevent albuterol (a beta-agonist) from working, potentially triggering severe bronchospasms in patients with respiratory conditions.
Concomitant use may result in significantly increased potassium levels in the blood (hyperkalemia).
